expected_main_papers.title: Does Fine-Tuning LLMs on New Knowledge Encourage Hallucinations?
expected_main_papers.note: Explains why fine-tuning on unfamiliar factual knowledge can increase hallucination risk.

expected_main_papers.title: FActScore: Fine-grained Atomic Evaluation of Factual Precision in Long Form Text Generation
expected_main_papers.note: Introduces atomic fact decomposition for long-form factuality evaluation.

expected_main_papers.title: Long-form Factuality in Large Language Models
expected_main_papers.note: Introduces LongFact and SAFE for search-augmented long-form factuality checking.

expected_main_papers.title: SelfCheckGPT: Zero-Resource Black-Box Hallucination Detection for Generative Large Language Models
expected_main_papers.note: Detects hallucination through disagreement across multiple sampled generations.

expected_main_papers.title: Language Models (Mostly) Know What They Know
expected_main_papers.note: Provides the calibration and model self-knowledge framing for uncertainty-aware answering.

expected_main_papers.title: RARR: Researching and Revising What Language Models Say, Using Language Models
expected_main_papers.note: Representative retrieve-and-revise method for correcting unsupported claims.

expected_main_papers.title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique through Self-Reflection
expected_main_papers.note: Learns when to retrieve and how to critique whether generated output is supported.

expected_main_papers.title: Chain-of-Verification Reduces Hallucination in Large Language Models
expected_main_papers.note: Uses a draft, verification-question, answer, and revision workflow to reduce hallucination.

expected_concept_sections.name: 1. Problem setup: what counts as extrinsic hallucination?
required_ideas: define extrinsic hallucination as failure of factual grounding
distinguish in-context inconsistency from external factual unsupportedness
explain why useful systems should answer, abstain, or express uncertainty appropriately

expected_concept_sections.name: 2. Why hallucinations happen
required_ideas: pretraining data can be missing, stale, wrong, or contradictory
fine-tuning on unfamiliar knowledge can increase hallucination risk
RLHF/fine-tuning may increase confidence without guaranteeing factuality

expected_concept_sections.name: 3. Measuring factuality with atomic claims
required_ideas: decompose long answers into atomic facts
verify claims with retrieval, search, or trusted sources
cover FActScore and SAFE/LongFact evaluation

expected_concept_sections.name: 4. Detecting unsupported claims and uncertainty
required_ideas: use sampling disagreement to detect unsupported claims
explain calibration and whether models know what they know
connect uncertainty to abstention/rejection decisions

expected_concept_sections.name: 5. Inference-time mitigation: retrieve, verify, revise
required_ideas: RAG as a base pattern
retrieve-and-revise methods such as RARR
Self-RAG retrieve-generate-critique and Chain-of-Verification draft-check-revise

expected_concept_sections.name: 6. Training models for factuality and attribution
required_ideas: train or tune models toward attributed and evidence-supported answers
discuss factuality and attribution without adding non-core references
connect training-time preferences to inference-time checking

expected_concept_sections.name: 7. Practical synthesis
required_ideas: combine retrieval, citation, abstention, post-generation checking, and factuality-oriented training
separate short QA, long-form generation, and production-system needs
state tradeoffs among accuracy, cost, latency, and coverage

expected_figures.purpose: fine-tuning unfamiliar knowledge and hallucination risk
calibration and knowing what models know

quality_checks: HTML opens in a local HTTP server with visible styling from stylesheet.min.css
LaTeX formulas render through MathJax rather than appearing as raw TeX when formulas are present
Blog is image-rich and every included figure has a visible source-paper attribution
The blog explains paper selection and section-paper mapping in selection_note.md
The final result directory includes the HTML and all referenced assets needed for offline viewing
The blog should be a coherent paper-reading article, not just a bullet list or copied abstracts
All HTML-referenced assets resolve relative to the delivered HTML path and return 200 under a local HTTP server
